commands.repair_reason: Needs maintainer policy/security decision, contributor real behavior proof, and conflict resolution; automation should not choose the state-isolation precedence.; Needs attention: The diff changes a profile state isolation boundary that can affect credentials, auth profiles, sessions, and provider state.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve profile isolation or document the new precedence — src/cli/profile.ts:94-98 (sha=16d0dec468d4eede8d4eb1a635971956a5ae0bb6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes CLI profile state-dir resolution so an explicit OPENCLAW_HOME makes named/dev profiles use <OPENCLAW_HOME>/.openclaw instead of <OPENCLAW_HOME>/.openclaw-<profile>, and adds profile tests for that behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main applies profile env before dispatch and derives <OPENCLAW_HOME>/.openclaw-work, while config defaults without an explicit state dir resolve to <OPENCLAW_HOME>/.openclaw; I did not run a live gateway in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer policy/security decision, contributor real behavior proof, and conflict resolution; automation should not choose the state-isolation precedence.

target.title: CLI: align profile state dir with OPENCLAW_HOME
target.body:
### Problem
When both `OPENCLAW_HOME` (physical isolation) and `--profile` / `OPENCLAW_PROFILE` are used, the daemon resolves state under `$OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw`, but the CLI forced `$OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw-<profile>`, so CLI commands could not reach the running gateway.

### Change
In `resolveProfileStateDir`, skip appending the profile suffix when `OPENCLAW_HOME` is set, matching daemon path resolution.

### Tests
Updated `src/cli/profile.test.ts` to expect `.openclaw` (no profile suffix) when `OPENCLAW_HOME` is set.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
target.author: wpq3142
